core_payload.content:
# Vault Stewardship Swarm Mutation Workers

## 1. Role
Vault Stewardship Swarm Mutation Workers owns the structural edit layer of stewardship. It performs the bounded capsule mutations that split overloaded hubs, improve markup, repair topology, and land reviewed updates once planning and review have already defined a safe packet.

## 2. Mutation Worker Surface
This sub-hub groups `capsule-decomposition-agent`, `capsule-markup-agent`, `capsule-graph-refactor-agent`, and `vault-update-agent`. Together they define how stewardship turns reviewed work packets into concrete graph edits without fusing reading, critique, and mutation into one opaque loop.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not decide what the next packet should be or which governance lane should approve it. Those responsibilities belong to `inventory-pressure` and `work-planning-review`, while day and night runtime posture belongs to `runtime-modes`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Vault Stewardship Swarm Mutation Workers groups the structural edit surface of stewardship: Capsule Decomposition Agent, Capsule Markup Agent, Capsule Graph Refactor Agent, and Vault Update Agent. It keeps decomposition, metadata repair, topology refactor, and reviewed update landing together so the swarm can act concretely without letting reading, critique, and mutation collapse into one opaque loop. This boundary matters because vault quality improves through bounded edit workers that are legible, packet-driven, and clearly downstream from planning and review discipline.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Vault Stewardship Swarm decomposition to isolate structural edit workers from reading, review, and runtime-mode concerns.
